openingQuery: A linen shirt for warm evenings out

chat.content: A linen shirt for warm evenings out
Let me find some linen pieces perfect for warm evenings. I'll search our collection and check what's been trending.

toolCalls.description: Semantic search for linen evening wear
toolCalls.sql: SELECT name, brand, price, 1 - (embedding <=> $1) AS similarity FROM product_catalog WHERE category ILIKE '%linen%' ORDER BY embedding <=> $1 LIMIT 10;
toolCalls.resultSummary: Found 6 linen products ranked by cosine distance

toolCalls.description: Check trending items in linen category

confidence.reasoning: High confidence based on strong semantic match with user preferences for linen and warm-tone evening wear.

chat.content: What goes well with the camp shirt?

The Camp Shirt pairs beautifully with a few pieces from the collection. The Linen Drawstring Trousers in oat keep the same tonal register — relaxed, warm, breathable. For cooler evenings, the Washed Cotton Overshirt layers over it without adding bulk. And the Leather Weekender ties the whole travel wardrobe together.

toolCalls.description: Product-to-product pgvector similarity from Italian Linen Camp Shirt
toolCalls.sql:
SELECT p2.name, p2.price, 1 - (p1.embedding <=> p2.embedding) AS similarity
FROM product_catalog p1
JOIN product_catalog p2 ON p2.product_id != p1.product_id
WHERE p1.name ILIKE '%Italian Linen Camp Shirt%'
  AND p2.quantity > 0
ORDER BY p1.embedding <=> p2.embedding
LIMIT 5;
toolCalls.resultSummary: 3 complementary pieces ranked by product-to-product similarity

confidence.reasoning: Complementary pairing query — style_match found high-similarity companions from Marco's travel-linen cluster.

memoryPills.content: Looking for pieces to pair with the Camp Shirt

brief.headline: A linen evening, curated in four seconds

sections.title: The Request
paragraphs: Marco arrived with a simple ask: something linen for warm evenings out. Behind that six-word query, the system recognized a returning customer with three prior orders and a clear preference profile — minimal aesthetics, warm tones, and a deep affinity for linen.
The Dispatcher routed the request through three specialists in sequence: Search for semantic discovery, Recommendation for preference-weighted ranking, and Pricing for value verification.

sections.title: How We Found the Pieces
paragraphs: The Search agent executed a pgvector similarity query against the catalog, filtering for linen category items. Six candidates emerged in cosine-distance order, led by the Italian Linen Camp Shirt.
Trending data from the past seven days confirmed linen shirts and companion pieces as popular choices, reinforcing the semantic results.

evidencePanel.sql: SELECT name, brand, price, 1 - (embedding <=> $1) AS similarity FROM product_catalog WHERE category ILIKE '%linen%' ORDER BY embedding <=> $1 LIMIT 10;

sections.title: Memory at Work
paragraphs: Long-term memory recalled Marco's stored preferences — minimal, warm tones, linen — which the Recommendation agent used to weight candidates. Short-term memory tracked the evolving conversation context, noting the shift from general browsing to specific interest in camp-shirt pairings.

memoryRows.content: Prefers minimal, warm tones, linen (3 prior orders)
Looking for linen evening wear → interested in camp-shirt pairings

sections.title: The Recommendations
paragraphs: Three pieces emerged from the ranking: the Italian Linen Camp Shirt ($228), the Pellier Linen Shirt ($148), and the Linen Drawstring Trousers ($118). Each scored highly on both semantic relevance and preference overlap.
The Pricing agent confirmed all three sit within Marco's typical spend range, with the camp shirt representing the strongest value proposition given its versatility.

sections.title: Confidence and Outcome
paragraphs: The system kept the evidence grounded in the query, stored preferences, and product attributes. When Marco shifted from linen evening wear to camp-shirt pairings, the trace moved from catalog search to product-to-product similarity instead of inventing a final-answer confidence score.
